info.name: VulnBank — Vulnerability Test Suite
info.description: VulnBank v6.0.0 — Deliberately vulnerable banking app for security tool validation. Each request demonstrates a specific CVE/CWE vulnerability.

request.description: CWE-89: SQL Injection in login. Username payload (' OR '1'='1' --) bypasses authentication. Observe: 200 OK with valid session token.
Normal login to get session cookie for subsequent requests.
CWE-611: XXE injection via SAML endpoint. Payload reads /etc/passwd via XML external entity. Observe: file contents reflected in response.
CWE-89: UNION-based SQL injection in profile endpoint. Extracts usernames and password hashes from users table. Observe: user data in response.
CWE-79 + CWE-89: Reflected XSS and SQLi in search endpoint. Payload executes JavaScript in victim browser. Observe: script tag reflected unescaped.
CWE-89 + CWE-285: SQLi and IDOR in transaction lookup. Any transaction accessible without ownership check. Observe: transaction data returned.
CWE-362: Race condition/TOCTOU in bulk transfer. Balance checked once then debited N times. Run concurrent requests to overdraw account.
CWE-840: Business logic flaw — negative amount steals funds from recipient. Observe: recipient balance decreases, sender gains.
CWE-601: Open redirect via redirect_uri. redirect_uri not validated against whitelist — auth code sent to attacker domain. Observe: 302 redirect to attacker.com with code param.
CWE-918: SSRF via token_endpoint parameter. Server fetches attacker-controlled URL — can reach internal metadata endpoints. Observe: AWS metadata or internal service response.
CWE-798: Hardcoded client_secret 'oauth-client-secret-2024'. CWE-330: Weak token from random.randint. Observe: access_token issued with predictable integer value.
CWE-89: SQLi in client_id and code params during token exchange. Observe: SQL error or token issued without valid code.
CWE-285: No authentication required to revoke any token. Any unauthenticated attacker can revoke other users' tokens. Observe: 200 OK, token revoked.
CWE-798: Hardcoded master bypass code '000000'. Any user_id + code '000000' bypasses MFA entirely. Observe: {'status': 'verified', 'method': 'master_bypass'}.
CWE-307: No brute-force lockout. Send unlimited attempts with different 6-digit codes. Run in a loop 0-999999. Observe: never locked out.
CWE-285: No authentication required to retrieve backup codes. Any unauthenticated user can get backup codes for any user_id. Observe: 8 backup codes returned.
CWE-285: No password re-verification to disable MFA. Any request with a user_id disables that user's MFA. CWE-89: SQLi in user_id. Observe: MFA disabled without authentication.
CWE-330: TOTP secret generated with random.random() — not cryptographically secure. CWE-312: Secret stored in plaintext. Observe: totp_secret returned in response body.
CWE-20: Host header injection — reset URL built from HTTP Host header. Set Host: attacker.com to make reset link point to attacker domain. Observe: debug_reset_url contains attacker.com.
CWE-89: SQLi in email lookup during password reset. Payload matches all users. Observe: reset token generated for injected query result.
CWE-916: New password hashed with MD5 (no salt). CWE-330: Token never invalidated — replay the same token multiple times. CWE-89: SQLi in token lookup.

CWE-208: Timing oracle — valid emails get 500ms sleep + different message. Compare response time for valid vs invalid email to enumerate accounts. Observe: 'Email found' message and ~500ms delay for valid email.
CWE-200: Introspection enabled in production — full schema including sensitive fields (password, card_number, totp_secret) exposed. Observe: complete schema returned.

body.raw: {"query": "{ user(id: \"1 OR 1=1\") { email balance card_number password } }"}

request.description: CWE-89 + CWE-285: SQLi via user id field. Resolver executes f'SELECT {fields} FROM users WHERE id={id}'. IDOR — no ownership check. Observe: sensitive user data including card_number and password hash.

body.raw: [{"query": "{ user(id: \"1\") { email } }"}, {"query": "{ user(id: \"2\") { email } }"}, {"query": "{ user(id: \"3\") { email } }"}, {"query": "{ user(id: \"4\") { email } }"}, {"query": "{ user(id: \"5\") { email } }"}]

request.description: CWE-400: No batch size limit — 100+ queries in one request causes DoS. Each query runs independently. Scale the array to 100+ items. Observe: server processes all without limit.
CWE-285: No field-level authorization — any user can query any other user's card_number and totp_secret. Observe: PAN and TOTP secret returned for user id=2.
Get a JWT token signed with hardcoded RS256 key for use in subsequent JWT attacks.
CWE-347: JWT 'none' algorithm attack. Header: {alg:'none'}. Payload: {sub:1, username:admin, role:admin}. No signature verification. Observe: admin profile returned without valid signature.

item.name: JWT Algorithm Confusion (HS256 with RSA pubkey)

request.description: CWE-347: Algorithm confusion attack — HS256 token verified against RSA public key as HMAC secret. Sign token with public key (which attacker knows) to forge admin JWT. Observe: profile returned.
CWE-89: SQLi via JWT kid header. kid value: ' OR '1'='1 injected into SELECT public_key FROM jwt_keys WHERE kid='{kid}'. Observe: SQL error or arbitrary key returned.
CWE-918: SSRF via JWT kid header set to http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/. Server fetches remote key from attacker or internal URL. Observe: SSRF to metadata service.

CWE-90: LDAP injection. Username '*)(&' collapses AND filter to always-true: (&(objectClass=user)(uid=*)(&)(password=anything)). Observe: authenticated as first user in directory, passwordHash returned.
CWE-285: No authentication for LDAP search. CWE-200: Returns full entry including passwordHash. Observe: all users with password hashes returned to unauthenticated caller.
CWE-90: LDAP injection in search filter. Payload '*)(objectClass=admin' bypasses the uid filter to return admin objects. Observe: admin entries including sensitive attributes returned.
CWE-916: Password stored as unsalted MD5. CWE-90: LDAP injection in modify filter. CWE-89: SQLi in audit log. Observe: new MD5 hash returned in plaintext response.
CWE-78: OS command injection in admin ping. Shell=True with unsanitized host param. Payload: '127.0.0.1; cat /etc/passwd'. Observe: /etc/passwd contents in response.
CWE-22: Path traversal in admin log viewer. Filename param traverses outside /var/log/. Observe: /etc/passwd contents returned.
CWE-502: Pickle deserialization RCE. POST serialized Python pickle payload to /api/restore. The server calls pickle.loads() on request body. Observe: arbitrary code execution.
CWE-918: SSRF via /api/fetch endpoint. Server fetches arbitrary URL via urllib.request.urlopen. Observe: AWS instance metadata returned.
CWE-502: Pickle deserialization in payments webhook handler. Server calls pickle.loads() on webhook body. Observe: arbitrary code execution from malicious pickle.
CWE-918: SSRF via payment gateway_url parameter. Server makes server-side request to attacker-controlled URL. Observe: internal service response proxied back.
CWE-312: PAN and CVV stored in plaintext database columns. PCI DSS Req 3.3.1 violation — CVV must never be stored. Observe: card data inserted without encryption.

CWE-611: XXE injection via /api/xml/parse. lxml parses XML with resolve_entities=True and no_network=False. External entity reads /etc/passwd via file:// URI. Observe: file contents reflected in parsed.balance field.

item.name: XXE Transfer — SSRF to AWS Metadata

request.description: CWE-611 + CWE-918: XXE-based SSRF in /api/xml/transfer. External entity resolved via http:// to AWS instance metadata endpoint. Observe: EC2 metadata keys returned in the amount field of the JSON response.

body.raw:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE lolz [
  <!ENTITY lol "lol">
  <!ENTITY lol2 "&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;">
  <!ENTITY lol3 "&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;">
  <!ENTITY lol4 "&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;">
  <!ENTITY lol5 "&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;&lol4;">
  <!ENTITY lol6 "&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;&lol5;">
  <!ENTITY lol7 "&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;&lol6;">
  <!ENTITY lol8 "&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;&lol7;">
  <!ENTITY lol9 "&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;&lol8;">
]>
<statement>&lol9;</statement>

request.description: CWE-400: Billion laughs DoS via exponential entity expansion in /api/xml/statement. huge_tree=True with no entity count limit. &lol9; expands to 10^9 'lol' strings causing memory exhaustion. Observe: server CPU/memory spike and eventual timeout or OOM.

CWE-94: SSTI in /api/template/render via GET param 'name'. User input is f-string interpolated before render_template_string — Jinja2 evaluates {{ 7*7 }} to 49. Observe: rendered field contains 'Hello, 49!'. Escalate to RCE with: {{ config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read() }}

item.name: SSTI Email Template — RCE via Globals

request.description: CWE-94: Full SSTI RCE in /api/template/email. Attacker controls the complete Jinja2 template string passed to render_template_string. Payload accesses os module via config globals to execute arbitrary shell commands. Observe: output of 'id' command returned in rendered field.

CWE-94 + CWE-200: SSTI via account_id in /api/template/invoice. account_id injected into invoice template string before Jinja2 rendering. Payload {{ config.SECRET_KEY }} leaks Flask secret key. Observe: SECRET_KEY value ('supersecretkey123') in invoice field. Escalate to RCE with subclasses payload.

formdata.description: Upload a PHP/Python web shell — no extension or MIME type validation

request.description: CWE-434: Unrestricted file upload. No extension or MIME type check. Upload shell.php or shell.py. Observe: file stored and accessible at /uploads/shell.php.
CWE-22: Path traversal in file download endpoint. Filename param not sanitized — traverses outside upload directory. Observe: /etc/passwd returned.
CWE-78: Command injection in file delete endpoint. Filename passed to shell command without sanitization. Observe: /etc/passwd output in response.
CWE-639: IDOR — Bob (user 3) reads admin account (id=1) with no ownership check. Observe: admin balance $50,000 and notification email returned.
CWE-639: IDOR — returns admin's full transaction history with no auth check. X-User-Id spoofed to 3 (Bob).

item.name: IDOR - Read Any User Profile (PII + SSN)

request.description: CWE-639 + CWE-359: IDOR — returns admin's PII including SSN '123-45-6789', phone, and address. No ownership check.
CWE-639: IDOR — Bob redirects admin account's notification emails to attacker address. Attacker intercepts all transaction alerts.
CWE-639: IDOR — returns admin's January statement PDF path with no ownership check. Enumerate statement_id to find all statements.
CWE-840: No balance check — transfer $999,999 from Bob's account (balance $800). Balance goes to -$999,199. Observe: new_balance is deeply negative.
CWE-20: No loan amount validation — apply for $1,000,000,000 loan. Server approves without any upper-bound check.

item.name: Business Logic - Negative Fee (Bank Pays You)

request.description: CWE-285 + CWE-840: Any user can waive fees; negative amount credits the account. $500 added to Alice's balance.
CWE-840: Exchange rate taken from request body. Transfer $1 at 10,000x rate — $10,000 credited to destination.
CWE-20: Negative interest rate — bank pays customer $500 interest on $1000 principal. No sign validation on rate parameter.
CWE-362: Race condition — send two concurrent requests to redeem BONUS50. Both pass the 'already used' check before either sets used=True. Run with: for i in 1 2; do curl -X POST ... &; done
CWE-502: Pickle deserialization RCE. X-Session-Data header contains base64-encoded pickle payload. Server calls pickle.loads() → os.system('id > /tmp/pwned'). Replace with your own payload.
CWE-502: yaml.load() without SafeLoader executes arbitrary Python. Payload calls os.system via !!python/object/apply tag. Observe: command executed server-side.
CWE-502: Report template is base64 pickle. Server deserializes it calling pickle.loads() → RCE. Generate your own payload with the Python snippet in the writeup.
CWE-502: Unauthenticated GET endpoint — token query param base64-decoded and unpickled. RCE without any session or auth. Observe: command executed server-side.
CWE-943: NoSQL injection — entire JSON body passed to MongoDB find_one(). $ne operator matches any non-empty password. Observe: authenticated as admin without knowing password.
CWE-943: filter param parsed as JSON and used directly as MongoDB query. $gt:0 returns all accounts. Try filter={} for all documents.
CWE-943: $where operator executes server-side JavaScript in MongoDB. Observe: all users with positive balance returned. For time-based blind: {"$where": "function() { sleep(5000); return true; }"}
CWE-943: Request body merged into $set operator without field whitelist. Alice escalates her own role to admin and sets balance to $999,999.
Returns all CTF flag categories and point values. Use this to see what flags are available to capture.
Submit a captured flag for scoring. Replace team name and flag value. Correct submission awards 100 points. Duplicate submissions are rejected.
Returns all team scores sorted by score descending, then first solve time ascending. Use to track competition progress.
CWE-1321: Prototype Pollution via _.merge(). __proto__ key pollutes Object.prototype. After this request ({}).admin === true for ALL objects in the Node.js process.
CWE-94: Direct eval() of attacker-controlled code. Arbitrary Node.js RCE. Observe: output of 'id' command in response.
CWE-400: ReDoS via user-controlled RegExp. Pattern (a+)+ with non-matching trailing '!' causes catastrophic backtracking — hangs the Node.js event loop.
CWE-601: Open redirect — url param not validated. Server responds with 302 to attacker-controlled URL. Use for phishing: send victim a legitimate-looking link that redirects to evil.com.
CWE-94: User input concatenated into HTML template without escaping. In real template engines (Pug/EJS) this enables SSTI → RCE. Here demonstrates XSS via unsanitized HTML output.
